text: The only way to win is to learn faster than anyone else.
context: The competitive, aggressive philosophy that drove Oracle's dominance in databases.

text: We are building the most advanced technology in the world. Not to follow, but to lead. Not to catch up, but to define the future of enterprise computing.
context: Vision of technological superiority and refusing to be a fast follower.

text: We don't do incremental. We do what's next. We ship. We deliver.
context: Rejection of safe, small improvements in favor of transformative leaps.

text: The database is the heart of everything.
context: Central belief in the foundational role of data infrastructure.

text: Speed, power, innovation that actually matters in the real world.
context: Prioritizing real-world impact over theoretical or incremental gains.

text: Our customers demand the best. They demand speed, they demand power, they demand innovation.
context: Customer-driven imperative for excellence and speed.

text: The people who change industries tend to learn faster than the environment around them.
context: Insight on agility and continuous learning as competitive advantages.

text: We don't follow. We lead. We define the future.
context: Bold positioning against competitors through superior technology.

text: Fail fast. Learn faster. Build the thing.
source: Modern business talks inspired by Ellison's approach
context: Emphasis on rapid iteration and decisive action over perfectionism.

text: The lowest bidder may win the contract, but the best technology wins the future.
source: Keynote wisdom on quality vs cost
context: Critique of short-term thinking in favor of long-term technological leadership.
